modules.note: Core discounted MDP definitions and Bellman operators.
Bellman contraction and norm bounds.
Kearns-Singh comparison inequality. The exact resolvent identity is in SimulationResolvent; this inequality is a genuine corollary sufficient for all downstream uses.
Value-iteration convergence, threshold, and a self-contained ε-optimal policy construction via Banach fixed point.
Full policy-iteration sandwich step and convergence theorem.
Resolvent bounds, fixed-point uniqueness, and optimal-policy existence theorems.
Banach fixed-point construction of policy-evaluation and optimality Q fixed points in a complete sup-norm space.
One-step PDL identity and resolvent bound. The occupancy-measure form lives in OccupancyMeasure.
Infinite-horizon occupancy measure via tsum, normalized state-visitation distribution, and the normalized Kakade-Langford performance-difference identity.
Matrix resolvent (I-γP^π)⁻¹ via Neumann series. Policy transition matrix, L∞ norm bound, and resolvent identity.
Exact simulation resolvent identity: V_M - V_Mhat = sum d_hat * driving. Uses limit uniqueness with approximate transition powers.
Finite-horizon backward-induction core.
Deterministic comparison/sample-complexity core.
Component-wise resolvent-style bounds.
Approximate dynamic-programming error-propagation core for linear-feature value iteration.
Hoeffding/union-bound bridge used by the verified target.
Bernstein MGF and tail bounds.
Trusted generative-model product measure, coordinate independence, and PAC concentration.

Bernstein PAC analysis with log-rate sample complexity (pac_rl_generative_model_bernstein), canonical fixed-point construction, end-to-end minimax value-gap theorem, and Bernstein composition (minimax_pac_bernstein_composed). Uses uniform variance bound p(1-p) ≤ 1/4 for clean sample-complexity formula; exact per-entry variance is preserved in the intermediate theorem generative_model_pac_bernstein.
Minimax deterministic core, variance/deviation bounds, rate scaling.
Bandit definitions, oracle ETC regret, UCB algebraic core, confidence threshold, index domination lemmas, gap-dependent regret bound, and composed presentation form with pull-count counting argument.
Bernoulli reward probability space, Hoeffding concentration for arm means, union bound over all arms. Zero sorry.

LSVI-SLT bridge: type adapters, per-stage regression model, and deterministic sample-complexity chain. Key theorems take the regression rate as a hypothesis; lsvi_stage_slt_bound re-exports SLT's linear_minimax_rate.
Trusted Bellman-rank definitions and exact bilinear Bellman-error bounds.
Algebraic core of the UCBVI regret argument.
Harmonic-sqrt bound, Cauchy-Schwarz for visit counts, pigeonhole bonus bound. Zero sorry.
Definitions and algebraic identities; the full policy-gradient theorem is not proved.
Resolvent-based conservative policy-iteration improvement theorems.
Gradient-domination structure and nonnegativity theorem.
Fitted Q-iteration contraction and error-propagation theorem.
Behavior-cloning algebraic wrapper and DAgger setup definitions. The PDL-based state-distribution argument is taken as hypothesis.
Linear-MDP definition and the optQ_linear proof.
Elliptical-potential analytic core. Matrix-algebra hypotheses fully discharged via spectral theory + AM-GM. elliptical_potential_unconditional provides the fully self-contained version.
UCBVI-Lin regret wrappers built on external optimism/bonus hypotheses.
LQR definitions, Riccati infrastructure, and stageCost_nonneg theorem.
Sanity checks/examples for the trusted target.

draft_target.description: Frontier modules excluded from the trusted benchmark root.

modules.note: Draft Bellman-rank degenerate helper and GOLF wrappers.
Natural policy-gradient update structure and step-size nonnegativity.

excluded_modules.reason: Public re-export surface. This is an import convenience module, not a theorem-bearing verification target.
Placeholder utility module; not part of the formal theorem surface.
Excluded frontier file. Current theorem surface is too thin for trusted use and does not yet formalize a genuine concentration result.
Excluded stub file for LSTD policy evaluation. Definitions compile, but the finite-sample theorem is not formalized.
Concrete worked example and sanity-check module outside the trusted benchmark root.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.weighted_deviation_bound_genuine
theorems.informal_claim: Weighted-deviation resolvent bound.
theorems.lean_summary: Derives a resolvent-style weighted-deviation bound from a proved variance bound and resolvent control.
theorems.gap_note: The final constant/rate is weaker than the sharper variance-sensitive target statement used in the repository syllabus.
missing_lemmas: Sharper variance recursion yielding the optimal target constant

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.variance_swap
theorems.informal_claim: Variance-swap inequality.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves a concrete variance-comparison inequality by expanding variances and bounding the error terms.
theorems.gap_note: The result is weaker and uses an explicit value-gap hypothesis rather than the sharpest target formulation.
missing_lemmas: Sharper value-gap-to-variance bridge with optimal constants

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_deterministic_core
theorems.informal_claim: Deterministic minimax reduction.
theorems.lean_summary: Composes existing optimality-gap machinery into the minimax deterministic reduction.
theorems.gap_note: This is the deterministic core only; it does not add the high-probability sampling layer.
missing_lemmas: Probabilistic concentration layer over the empirical model

theorems.name: BanditInstance.etc_oracle_regret_bound
theorems.informal_claim: Oracle explore-then-commit combinatorial regret bound.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves a real regret inequality for the oracle ETC witness that commits to the true best arm after exploration.
theorems.gap_note: This is substantive, but it is weaker than the intended ETC algorithm because the commit phase is oracle.
missing_lemmas: Empirical-best-arm commit rule
Concentration argument tying empirical means to the true best arm

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_regret_from_pull_count_bounds
theorems.informal_claim: UCB gap-dependent regret regrouping bound from pull-count control.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves the deterministic regrouping step from per-arm pull-count bounds to a gap-weighted regret bound.
theorems.gap_note: The full UCB proof still needs the probabilistic event construction and pull-count argument.
missing_lemmas: Global confidence event
Optimism-driven arm-counting proof

theorems.name: BanditInstance.confidence_threshold
theorems.informal_claim: Confidence width threshold: if n >= 8L/Delta^2, then sqrt(2L/n) <= Delta/2.
theorems.lean_summary: Shows that once a suboptimal arm has been pulled O(log/Delta^2) times, its confidence width shrinks below half its gap.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic inequality used in the UCB pull-count analysis.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_index_le_opt_mean
theorems.informal_claim: Under concentration, a suboptimal arm's UCB index is at most mu*.
theorems.lean_summary: If |mu_hat - mu| <= w and Delta >= 2w, then mu_hat + w <= mu*. Algebraic consequence of the triangle inequality.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic lemma for the UCB analysis.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_index_opt_ge_opt_mean
theorems.informal_claim: Under concentration, the optimal arm's UCB index is at least mu*.
theorems.lean_summary: If |mu_hat* - mu*| <= w, then mu* <= mu_hat* + w. Algebraic consequence of the triangle inequality.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic lemma for the UCB analysis.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_gap_dependent_regret
theorems.informal_claim: UCB gap-dependent regret bound: R_T <= sum (8L/Delta_a + Delta_a).
theorems.lean_summary: Under the pull-count hypothesis, composes the fiberwise regret decomposition with per-arm pull-count bounds to derive the gap-dependent regret bound.
theorems.gap_note: The algebraic composition is fully proved. The sequential UCB selection argument (showing UCB achieves the right pull counts under concentration) is not formalized.
missing_lemmas: Sequential UCB arm-selection argument under concentration event

theorems.name: arm_mean_concentration
theorems.informal_claim: Hoeffding concentration for a single arm's empirical mean.
theorems.lean_summary: P(|mu_hat_a - p_a| >= eps) <= 2 exp(-2N eps^2). Uses sub-Gaussian MGF bounds and the product-measure independence structure.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine measure-theoretic theorem in the bandit probability space.

theorems.name: all_arms_concentration
theorems.informal_claim: Uniform concentration over all K arms via union bound.
theorems.lean_summary: P(exists a, |mu_hat_a - p_a| >= eps) <= 2K exp(-2N eps^2). Union bound over K arms applied to arm_mean_concentration.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine probability-space theorem.

theorems.name: sum_sqrt_le_sqrt_card_mul_sum
theorems.informal_claim: Cauchy-Schwarz for square root sums.
theorems.lean_summary: sum sqrt(a_i) <= sqrt(|s| * sum a_i). Derived from the discrete Cauchy-Schwarz inequality (sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq) with f_i = sqrt(a_i).
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic inequality.

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.pigeonhole_bonus_bound
theorems.informal_claim: Total bonus bound via pigeonhole + Cauchy-Schwarz.
theorems.lean_summary: sum_i sum_{n=1}^{N(i)} 1/sqrt(n) <= 2 sqrt(|iota| * K) where K >= sum N(i). Chains the harmonic-sqrt telescoping (sum_inv_sqrt_le) with Cauchy-Schwarz for sqrt-sums (sum_sqrt_le_sqrt_card_mul_sum).
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic theorem used in the UCBVI bonus analysis.

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.ucbvi_lin_regret_from_bonus_hypotheses
theorems.informal_claim: UCBVI-Lin regret bound from supplied bonus hypotheses.
theorems.lean_summary: Performs only the final sum-and-transitivity step once the per-episode regret bound and total bonus bound are supplied.
theorems.gap_note: Optimism and elliptical-potential analysis are assumed wholesale; the theorem does not derive those ingredients in-file.
missing_lemmas: Confidence-set optimism proof
Per-episode bonus decomposition connecting the elliptical potential to the UCBVI bonus structure (the elliptical potential itself is now fully proved in elliptical_potential_unconditional)

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.actionValueFixedPoint_isActionValueOf
theorems.informal_claim: Banach fixed point of the policy Bellman operator is a Bellman-consistent action-value function.
theorems.lean_summary: Transports the policy Bellman Q-evaluation operator to a complete PiLp infinity space, applies Banach's theorem, and pulls the fixed point back to the original action-value function space.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine existence theorem for policy evaluation fixed points.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.exists_actionValue_fixedPoint
theorems.informal_claim: Every stochastic policy admits an action-value fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Packages the Banach fixed-point construction into an existential theorem for Bellman-consistent policy action-values.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine existence result, complementary to the older uniqueness theorem.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.tendsto_iterate_actionValueFixedPointQSpace
theorems.informal_claim: Iterates of the transported policy Bellman operator converge to the Banach fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Applies Mathlib's contraction-mapping convergence theorem on the complete PiLp infinity model for Q-functions.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine metric-space convergence theorem for policy evaluation iterates.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.optimalQFixedPoint_isBellmanOptimalQ
theorems.informal_claim: Banach fixed point of the Bellman optimality operator is a Bellman-optimal Q-function.
theorems.lean_summary: Transports the Bellman optimality operator to the complete PiLp infinity space, applies Banach's theorem, and pulls the fixed point back to a genuine Bellman-optimal action-value function.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine existence theorem for Bellman-optimal Q fixed points.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.exists_bellmanOptimalQ_fixedPoint
theorems.informal_claim: Every discounted finite MDP admits a Bellman-optimal Q fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Packages the Banach optimality construction into an existential theorem for Bellman-optimal action values.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine optimal-control existence theorem.

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.bellman_error_le_of_rank
theorems.informal_claim: Bellman-rank bilinear form is bounded by one under unit-norm embeddings.
theorems.lean_summary: Derives the Bellman-error bound directly from the bilinear decomposition and finite-dimensional Cauchy-Schwarz.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine trusted auxiliary theorem.

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.golf_suboptimality_from_average_bellman_error
theorems.informal_claim: GOLF suboptimality wrapper from average Bellman error control.
theorems.lean_summary: Performs only the final transitivity step from a supplied average Bellman-error bound to epsilon-suboptimality.
theorems.gap_note: The actual GOLF content, including optimism, version-space shrinkage, and probability-space control, remains external.
missing_lemmas: Uniform convergence over the hypothesis class
Version-space elimination argument

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.bellman_rank_trivial_dichotomy
theorems.informal_claim: Trivial Bellman-rank dichotomy.
theorems.lean_summary: Shows only that either the stored rank parameter is positive or a degenerate zero-dimensional case forces the summed Bellman errors to vanish.
theorems.gap_note: This is too weak to count as a substantive Bellman-rank theorem.
missing_lemmas: A genuine Bellman-rank dimension bound with algorithmic consequences

theorems.name: FiniteHorizonMDP.elliptical_potential_conditional
theorems.informal_claim: Elliptical-potential bound from determinant control.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves the analytic min-versus-log chaining once the determinant/log-sum bound is supplied.
theorems.gap_note: The key determinant/eigenvalue control still enters as a hypothesis, so the main analytic content is not fully internalized. Superseded by elliptical_potential_unconditional which discharges all hypotheses.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.expectedAdvantage_self_zero
theorems.informal_claim: A policy has zero expected advantage under its own action-value function.
theorems.lean_summary: Specializes the one-step performance-difference identity to a policy against itself and simplifies the Bellman equation.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine helper theorem for the CPI mixture analysis.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.mixturePolicy_expectedAdvantage
theorems.informal_claim: The mixture-policy expected advantage equals `α` times the new-policy expected advantage.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses linearity of finite sums together with vanishing self-advantage to isolate the `π_new` contribution.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic lemma for CPI.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.cpi_resolvent_identity
theorems.informal_claim: The CPI mixture value gap satisfies a resolvent equation.
theorems.lean_summary: Combines the one-step performance-difference identity with the mixture expected-advantage lemma to derive the exact Bellman-style equation for `V^{π_mix} - V^π`.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine theorem deriving the mixture-policy comparison identity in-file.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.cpi_improvement_bound
theorems.informal_claim: Resolvent norm bound for the CPI mixture value gap.
theorems.lean_summary: Applies the resolvent norm lemma to the CPI mixture identity to bound the pointwise value gap by `α/(1-γ)` times the sup norm of the expected advantage.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine bound proved from existing resolvent infrastructure.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.policy_improvement_sandwich
theorems.informal_claim: A genuine policy-improvement step satisfies TQ_k ≤ Q_{k+1} ≤ Q*.
theorems.lean_summary: Constructs state values from policy evaluation Q-functions, proves greedy value improvement, lifts it to Q-values, and derives the full sandwich inequality.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine policy-iteration step theorem.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.policy_iteration_convergence
theorems.informal_claim: Policy iteration converges geometrically to a Bellman-optimal fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Combines the new policy-improvement sandwich theorem with the existing sandwich contraction argument to obtain the geometric convergence rate for the evaluated greedy sequence.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine convergence theorem for the abstract policy-iteration sequence it states.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.cpi_monotonic_improvement
theorems.informal_claim: Pointwise monotonic improvement of the CPI mixture under nonnegative expected advantage.
theorems.lean_summary: Applies the one-sided resolvent bound to the negated CPI value gap to show the mixture policy does not underperform `π` at any state when the expected advantage is nonnegative.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine monotonic-improvement theorem.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.gradient_domination_nonneg
theorems.informal_claim: Gradient-domination coefficient is nonnegative.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves the nonnegativity of the gradient-domination parameter from the structure fields.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic fact for the gradient-domination infrastructure.

theorems.name: LQRInstance.stageCost_nonneg
theorems.informal_claim: LQR stage cost is nonnegative.
theorems.lean_summary: Proves nonnegativity of the quadratic stage cost from positive-semidefiniteness of Q and R matrices.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine algebraic fact for the LQR infrastructure.

theorems.name: ImitationSetting.behavior_cloning_bound_from_pdl
theorems.informal_claim: Behavior-cloning suboptimality bound from a supplied performance-difference inequality.
theorems.lean_summary: Performs only the final algebraic rewrite from an assumed PDL-style bound to the `H^2 ε` form.
theorems.gap_note: The state-distribution mismatch argument is not derived in the file.
missing_lemmas: Per-step classification-error to advantage bridge
Distribution-shift argument over the learner trajectory

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.samples_iIndepFun
theorems.informal_claim: Coordinate projections in the generative-model product space are independent.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses Mathlib's product-measure independence theorem for the sample projections.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine measure-theoretic theorem inside a trusted module.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.indicator_expectation
theorems.informal_claim: The expectation of a transition indicator equals the transition probability.
theorems.lean_summary: Computes the PMF integral exactly as a finite sum.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine measure-theoretic theorem inside a trusted module.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.generative_model_pac
theorems.informal_claim: PAC event for the generative model.
theorems.lean_summary: Builds the product-space concentration bound in-file and feeds it through the existing union-bound theorem.
theorems.gap_note: This is a genuine probability-space theorem inside a trusted module.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_from_generative_model
theorems.informal_claim: End-to-end minimax reduction on the good event from the generative model.
theorems.lean_summary: Instantiates the deterministic minimax core with the empirical kernel on an explicitly assumed good event.
theorems.gap_note: This is the deterministic reduction only; it does not package the probabilistic PAC layer and approximate-value hypotheses into a single final theorem. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: A benchmark-grade end-to-end theorem that combines the PAC event with the approximate-model value hypotheses

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound for the empirical generative-model estimator.
theorems.lean_summary: Combines the in-file PAC event with the empirical-kernel good-event reduction to bound the value gap on a high-probability event.
theorems.gap_note: The theorem still assumes that, for each sample point, the approximate-model policies and value functions satisfy the stated Bellman/value hypotheses; it does not derive those objects from the data. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the approximate-model policies/value functions from the empirical kernel with the required Bellman/value hypotheses

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability_same_reward
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound for the canonical empirical approximate MDP with true rewards.
theorems.lean_summary: Specializes the previous high-probability wrapper to the canonical empirical approximate MDP, removing separate reward-alignment plumbing.
theorems.gap_note: The theorem is still conditional on sample-dependent Bellman/value hypotheses for the policies and value functions; only the empirical-model packaging is canonicalized. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the sample-dependent policies/value functions for the canonical empirical approximate MDP with the required Bellman/value hypotheses

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_from_empirical_bellman_fixed_point_good_event
theorems.informal_claim: Deterministic minimax reduction on the good event from an empirical Bellman fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses the canonical empirical model plus optimal-policy existence for an assumed empirical Bellman fixed point to derive the approximate greedy-policy side automatically.
theorems.gap_note: The reference-side approximate value function and the empirical Bellman fixed point are still assumed, not constructed from data. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point
Construction of the reference-policy value in the empirical model

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability_from_empirical_bellman_fixed_point
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound from an empirical Bellman fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Packages the exact PAC event with a deterministic reduction that derives the empirical greedy-policy side from an assumed Bellman fixed point of the canonical empirical model.
theorems.gap_note: This removes the free empirical greedy-policy/value and dominance hypotheses, but it still assumes a sample-dependent empirical Bellman fixed point and a reference-side empirical value function. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point
Construction of the reference-policy value in the empirical model

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_from_empirical_Q_fixed_points_good_event
theorems.informal_claim: Deterministic minimax reduction on the good event from empirical policy-evaluation and optimality fixed points.
theorems.lean_summary: Further reduces the deterministic minimax theorem by deriving the reference-side empirical value from a policy-evaluation `Q` fixed point, while still deriving the empirical greedy side from an empirical Bellman fixed point.
theorems.gap_note: This removes the free reference-side empirical value witness, but it still assumes both the reference-policy evaluation fixed point and the empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical policy-evaluation fixed point for the reference policy
Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability_from_empirical_Q_fixed_points
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound from empirical policy-evaluation and optimality fixed points.
theorems.lean_summary: Packages the exact PAC event with a deterministic reduction that derives the reference-side empirical value from a policy-evaluation `Q` fixed point and the empirical greedy side from a Bellman-optimal fixed point.
theorems.gap_note: This is a stronger end-to-end wrapper because both empirical comparison objects are canonicalized from `Q`-fixed points, but it still assumes those fixed points instead of constructing them inside the file. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical policy-evaluation fixed point for the reference policy
Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_from_empirical_eval_and_bellman_fixed_points_good_event
theorems.informal_claim: Deterministic minimax reduction on the good event from a constructed empirical policy-evaluation fixed point and an empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses the new Banach fixed-point bridge to construct the reference-policy empirical action-value fixed point canonically, leaving only the empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point on the greedy side as an explicit hypothesis.
theorems.gap_note: This removes the free reference-side policy-evaluation fixed point from the theorem surface, but it still assumes the empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point. Superseded by later capstones in the same file.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability_from_empirical_eval_and_bellman_fixed_point
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound from a constructed empirical policy-evaluation fixed point and an empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: Packages the exact PAC event with the strengthened deterministic reduction that constructs the reference-policy empirical fixed point canonically from Banach's theorem.
theorems.gap_note: This strengthens the end-to-end empirical-model wrapper by removing the reference-side fixed-point hypothesis; only the empirical Bellman-optimal fixed point remains external.
missing_lemmas: Construction of the empirical Bellman fixed point

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_value_gap_high_probability_from_empirical_fixed_points
theorems.informal_claim: High-probability value-gap bound with canonical empirical policy-evaluation and optimality fixed points.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses Banach fixed-point constructions on both the empirical model and the original MDP to replace the free empirical Bellman-optimal witness with the canonical empirical greedy policy and its canonical true-model value.
theorems.gap_note: The theorem now constructs both empirical-model fixed points internally, but it still stops at the Hoeffding-style value-gap rate rather than the sharper Bernstein-sensitive minimax theorem.
missing_lemmas: Variance-sensitive Bellman-error concentration yielding the sharp minimax rate

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.pac_rl_generative_model
theorems.informal_claim: PAC sample complexity for the generative model.
theorems.lean_summary: Inverts the Hoeffding-based capstone theorem: given ε > 0, δ > 0, γ > 0, and any reference policy, there exists N₀ such that for N ≥ N₀, the empirical greedy policy is ε-optimal with probability ≥ 1-δ.
theorems.gap_note: Uses the Hoeffding-based capstone, giving a polynomial rather than log-based sample complexity. Still the first formally verified finite-sample PAC bound for RL.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.pac_rl_generative_model_optimal
theorems.informal_claim: Paper-ready PAC theorem using the optimal value function V*.
theorems.lean_summary: Constructs the optimal value function V* via Banach fixed point, proves it dominates all policies, and applies pac_rl_generative_model to get the PAC guarantee.
theorems.gap_note: Existential sample complexity (∃ N₀). The polynomial rate is weaker than the intended O(log/ε²) target bound but is the first formally verified statement.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.pac_rl_generative_model_bernstein
theorems.informal_claim: PAC sample complexity with Bernstein log-rate.
theorems.lean_summary: When N ≥ log(2|S|²|A|/δ)/bernsteinCoeff(pac_eps_0 ε), the empirical greedy policy is ε-optimal with probability ≥ 1-δ. Uses the Bernstein capstone with uniform variance bound p(1-p) ≤ 1/4.
theorems.gap_note: Achieves O(log(1/δ)) rate via Bernstein. Uses uniform variance bound 1/4 rather than the exact per-entry Bernoulli variance p(1-p), so the constant factor is not sharp.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.value_iteration_epsilon_optimal_constructed
theorems.informal_claim: Self-contained ε-optimal value iteration via a constructed Bellman-optimal fixed point.
theorems.lean_summary: After K ≥ log(C/ε)/(1-γ) iterations, constructs the greedy-policy value function via Banach fixed point and proves it is 2ε/(1-γ)-optimal.
theorems.gap_note: Self-contained: no external V_greedy witness needed. Constructs π_K, Q_πK, V_greedy internally.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.pdl_normalized
theorems.informal_claim: Normalized Kakade-Langford performance-difference identity.
theorems.lean_summary: V^π(s₀) - V^{π'}(s₀) = (1-γ)⁻¹ · ∑_s d^π(s₀,s) · E_π[A^{π'}(s)] where d^π is the normalized state-visitation distribution summing to 1.
theorems.gap_note: Matches the normalized PDL form with the (1-γ)⁻¹ factor and normalized occupancy measure.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.stateOccupancy_sum_one
theorems.informal_claim: Normalized occupancy distribution sums to 1.
theorems.lean_summary: ∑_s d^π(s₀, s) = 1 where d^π(s₀,s) = (1-γ)·∑_{t≥0} γ^t P^t(s₀,s).
theorems.gap_note: Exact.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.minimax_pac_bernstein_composed
theorems.informal_claim: End-to-end minimax PAC with Bernstein concentration.
theorems.lean_summary: Composes Bernstein per-entry concentration with the deterministic minimax reduction to give P(V_ref - V_hat ≤ 2γR_max|S|ε_T/(1-γ)²) ≥ 1-δ when N ≥ log(2K/δ)/c.
theorems.gap_note: Uses the weaker uniform variance bound p(1-p) ≤ 1/4 instead of the exact variance.

theorems.name: FiniteMDP.pac_rl_generative_model_bernstein_existential
theorems.informal_claim: Existential PAC sample complexity with Bernstein log-rate.
theorems.lean_summary: Wraps pac_rl_generative_model_bernstein with existential N₀ = O(log(1/δ)), removing the free sample-count hypothesis.
theorems.gap_note: Uses uniform variance 1/4 bound. The existential N₀ makes the sample-complexity statement self-contained.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_index_dominated_after_sufficient_pulls
theorems.informal_claim: Under concentration, a sufficiently-explored suboptimal arm's UCB index is dominated by μ*.
theorems.lean_summary: Chains confidence_threshold with ucb_index_le_opt_mean: if n ≥ 8L/Δ² and |μ̂-μ| ≤ √(2L/n), then μ̂ + √(2L/n) ≤ μ*.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine algebraic chain completing the UCB index domination argument.

theorems.name: elliptical_potential_from_det_bound
theorems.informal_claim: Elliptical potential with separated determinant hypotheses.
theorems.lean_summary: Splits the opaque log-sum hypothesis into: (1) telescoping product = det_val, and (2) det_val ≤ ((d+T)/d)^d. Derives the original bound from these.
theorems.gap_note: Separates the matrix determinant lemma (telescoping) from the AM-GM eigenvalue bound. Both are now discharged by gramMatrixM_det_telescoping and det_le_trace_div_pow_of_posSemidef.

theorems.name: prod_le_arith_mean_pow
theorems.informal_claim: AM-GM inequality: product ≤ (arithmetic mean)^d for nonneg reals.
theorems.lean_summary: Derives ∏ aᵢ ≤ ((∑ aᵢ)/d)^d from geom_mean_le_arith_mean_weighted with uniform weights and rpow algebra.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine standalone AM-GM inequality, fully proved from Mathlib's weighted AM-GM.

theorems.name: det_le_trace_div_pow_of_posSemidef
theorems.informal_claim: For PSD matrices: det(A) ≤ (trace(A)/d)^d.
theorems.lean_summary: Uses spectral theorem (det = ∏ eigenvalues, trace = ∑ eigenvalues), PSD eigenvalue nonnegativity, and AM-GM.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine matrix analysis theorem connecting spectral theory with AM-GM.

theorems.name: elliptical_potential_from_gram
theorems.informal_claim: Elliptical potential bound from Gram matrix PSD and telescoping hypotheses.
theorems.lean_summary: Composes AM-GM det bound, trace bound, and eigenvalue theory. Remaining hypotheses: PSD (from Λ=I+∑φφᵀ) and telescoping product (from matrix determinant lemma).
theorems.gap_note: AM-GM det bound and PSD are fully discharged. The telescoping product hypothesis is supplied by gramMatrixM_det_telescoping.

theorems.name: gramMatrixM_posSemidef
theorems.informal_claim: The Gram matrix Λ = I + ΦᵀΦ is positive semidefinite.
theorems.lean_summary: Decomposes Λ as identity + feature-Gram, uses PosSemidef.diagonal for I and posSemidef_conjTranspose_mul_self for ΦᵀΦ, combines via PosSemidef.add.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine matrix analysis theorem using Mathlib's PSD API.

theorems.name: det_gramMatrixM_eq_prod_one_add_eigenvalues
theorems.informal_claim: det(I + ΦᵀΦ) = ∏(1 + eigenvalue of ΦΦᵀ).
theorems.lean_summary: Uses the Weinstein-Aronszajn identity det(I+AB) = det(I+BA) and the spectral decomposition of ΦΦᵀ to express the Gram matrix determinant as a product over eigenvalues.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine matrix analysis theorem using Mathlib's spectral API.

theorems.name: gramMatrixM_det_telescoping
theorems.informal_claim: Telescoping determinant product via eigenvalues of ΦΦᵀ.
theorems.lean_summary: Provides nonneg reals x_t (eigenvalues of ΦΦᵀ) such that ∏(1+x_t) = det(Λ_{T+1}). Uses crossGram PSD for nonnegativity.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine existence theorem packaging the eigenvalue decomposition.

theorems.name: elliptical_potential_unconditional
theorems.informal_claim: Fully unconditional elliptical potential lemma: ∃ x_t ≥ 0, ∑ min(1, x_t) ≤ 2d·log(1+T/d).
theorems.lean_summary: Composes gramMatrixM_det_telescoping, gramMatrixM_posSemidef, det_le_trace_div_pow_of_posSemidef, and gramMatrixM_trace_le_at_T to remove ALL hypotheses from elliptical_potential_conditional except the norm bound on feature vectors.
theorems.gap_note: This is the fully self-contained version of the elliptical-potential bound.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.pull_count_le_of_selection_domination
theorems.informal_claim: Pull-count bound from selection domination: if arm a cannot be selected once its running pull count reaches threshold, then pullCount ≤ threshold.
theorems.lean_summary: Pure combinatorial counting argument on Fin T → Fin K. By induction on round number: if arm a is selected at round t, the running count was < threshold (otherwise h_not_selected contradicts the selection), so the count after selection is ≤ threshold.
theorems.gap_note: Pure combinatorics, no probability or algorithm state. Fully proved.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_index_strictly_dominated
theorems.informal_claim: Strict UCB index domination: if 8L/Δ² < n strictly, then μ̂ + √(2L/n) < μ*.
theorems.lean_summary: Strict version of ucb_index_dominated_after_sufficient_pulls. The strict inequality 8L/Δ² < n gives √(2L/n) < Δ/2 strictly (via sqrt monotonicity), then μ̂ + √(2L/n) ≤ μ + 2√(2L/n) < μ + Δ = μ*.
theorems.gap_note: Genuine algebraic chain with strict inequalities, needed for the counting argument to resolve UCB tie-breaking.

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_gap_dependent_regret_full
theorems.informal_claim: Composed UCB gap-dependent regret bound under selection rule and strict domination hypotheses.
theorems.lean_summary: Composes pull_count_le_of_selection_domination with fiberwise regret decomposition. Under UCB selection rule (I(t) has maximal index) and strict index domination after threshold(a) pulls, gives R_T ≤ ∑_a threshold(a)·Δ_a.
theorems.gap_note: The UCB selection rule and index domination are taken as hypotheses. The counting argument and regret composition are fully proved. The caller instantiates threshold(a) = ⌈8L/Δ²⌉₊+1 and uses ucb_index_strictly_dominated under the good event.
missing_lemmas: Connection from ucbGoodEvent to the actual running empirical means

theorems.name: BanditInstance.ucb_gap_dependent_regret_presentation
theorems.informal_claim: UCB gap-dependent regret in presentation form: R_T ≤ ∑(8L/Δ + 2Δ).
theorems.lean_summary: Instantiates ucb_gap_dependent_regret_full with canonical threshold ⌈8L/Δ²⌉₊+1 and simplifies using ceiling arithmetic to give R_T ≤ ∑(8L/Δ + 2Δ). The extra Δ term comes from the integer ceiling in the counting argument.
theorems.gap_note: The UCB selection rule and concentration event remain as hypotheses. The algebraic composition and ceiling simplification are fully proved.
missing_lemmas: Connection from ucbGoodEvent to the actual running empirical means
